methodsAttempted.query: site:bg.ac.rs вештачка интелигенција академски интегритет Универзитет у Београду
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl Serbian-language search found the official Faculty of Organizational Sciences general guidelines DOCX for use of generative artificial intelligence.
Robots.txt disallows administrative and theme/plugin/cache paths but explicitly allows /wp-content/uploads/, where the DOCX guidance file is hosted.
Robots.txt only disallows /cache/; the public AI policy page was fetched with direct HTTP.
Central University of Belgrade robots.txt permits public crawling except /wp-admin/. Central regulations pages checked during discovery did not return an AI-specific source.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found faculty-level University of Belgrade sources: a Serbian Faculty of Organizational Sciences generative AI guidance document and the faculty journal's AI policy page. Claims are scoped to those sources and do not assert a central university-wide AI policy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Опште смернице за коришћење генеративне вештачке интелигенције на Факултету организационих наука

artifacts.claimText: The University of Belgrade Faculty of Organizational Sciences has general guidelines that frame responsible generative AI use for students, teaching staff, and non-teaching staff, aiming to improve academic integrity, transparency, and ethical conduct in research, teaching, professional work, and administration.

citation.citationTitle: Опште смернице за коришћење генеративне вештачке интелигенције на Факултету организационих наука
citation.publisher: University of Belgrade Faculty of Organizational Sciences
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Faculty of Organizational Sciences guidance states that generative AI use is permitted only with respect for academic integrity principles, copyright and intellectual property, personal-data protection, ethical principles, and confidentiality obligations.

citation.citationTitle: Опште смернице за коришћење генеративне вештачке интелигенције на Факултету организационих наука
citation.publisher: University of Belgrade Faculty of Organizational Sciences
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Faculty of Organizational Sciences guidance lists prohibited generative AI uses including academic-integrity violations, generating or spreading false content, copyright violations, and entering business or official secrets or other sensitive content into prompts.

citation.citationTitle: Опште смернице за коришћење генеративне вештачке интелигенције на Факултету организационих наука

artifacts.claimText: The Faculty of Organizational Sciences journal AI policy requires authors to disclose generative AI use in both the cover letter and a manuscript statement, forbids authors from including personal, confidential, or proprietary material in prompts, and says GAI must not be used to generate peer-review reports.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Смернице су развијене ради пружања оквира за одговорно коришћење ГВИ технологија код студената, наставног и ненаставног особља. Смернице имају за циљ да се унапреде академски интегритет, транспарентност и етичко понашање у истраживању, наставним пословима, стручним пословима и администрацији.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guidelines provide a framework for responsible generative AI use by students, teaching staff, and non-teaching staff, aiming to improve academic integrity, transparency, and ethical conduct in research, teaching, professional work, and administration.

artifacts.title: University of Belgrade staged AI policy evidence draft
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for University of Belgrade is limited to official Faculty of Organizational Sciences sources: a Serbian generative AI guidance document and a faculty journal AI policy. The draft should describe these as faculty-level and journal-specific findings, not as a central university-wide AI policy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No central University of Belgrade-wide AI policy was verified in this staged run; claims are scoped to Faculty of Organizational Sciences or its journal.
